etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 昆(昆) (kūn) -> mix; confuse
etymology.interpretation: The semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") suggests fluidity and combination, essential in the concept of mixing or blending substances like liquids. The phonetic component 昆(昆) (kūn) contributes the sound without affecting the meaning. Together, they represent the idea of substances being mixed or confused, akin to how waters merge and blend indistinctly.
etymology.simplification: The character 混 was simplified by retaining the 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") component while simplifying and simplifying other parts for ease of writing, yet its core meaning was preserved.

examples.chinese: 他总是把事情混在一起(他總是把事情混在一起)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì bǎ shìqing hùn zài yīqǐ.

examples.chinese: 不要把糖和盐混淆(不要把糖和鹽混淆)。
examples.pinyin: Bùyào bǎ táng hé yán hùnxiáo.

examples.chinese: 他试图在群体中混得很好(他試圖在群體中混得很好)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shìtú zài qúntǐ zhōng hùn de hěn hǎo.
examples.english: He tries to blend in well with the group.
